semanticCore: Settling down, dwelling, returning, matching, or bearing
primaryMeaning: To settle in a dwelling or return/bear
quranicRelevance: Providing a dwelling/settling someone (Bawwa'a - 'bawwa'na li-ibrahima'), and returning with/bearing a burden (Baa'a - 'wa ba'oo bi-ghadhabin').

keyDerivations.meaning: To settle, establish, assign a place, or lodge
To return (with), incur, bear, or draw upon oneself

figurativeOrRareUsages: وَبَاءُوا بِغَضَبٍ مِنَ اللَّهِ: And they returned with/incurred wrath from God (bearing a moral liability).
أَنْ تَبُوءَ بِإِثْمِي (5:29): That you bear/incur my sin (and your sin).
discardedNoise: Matching equal values in blood revenge (بواء).
Obscure details of camel skin tents.
summaryEn: The root denotes lodging and bearing liability, describing the settling of peoples in lands (Bawwa'a) and the incurrence of spiritual wrath (Baa'a).
summaryAr: يدل هذا الجذر على المباءة وتحمل المسؤولية، واصفاً إسكان وتمكين العباد (بوّأ) وتحمل الذنوب والغضب الأخروي (باء).
rawText: جُوْزُ بَوَّا , also written جَوْزُ بَوَّى: see art. جوز. ___ خَيْرُ بَوَّآءُ: see art. خير.
